criteria.name: keyboard_operable_archive_controls
criteria.description: Every interactive control — view toggles, period filter, name sort, vision simulation, undo/redo, selection checkboxes, batch actions, swatches, favorite flags, editor fields, export controls, and popup controls — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), each showing a visible focus indicator when focused.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: A persistent coachmark overlay intercepted real pointer interaction, so every required control could not be exercised end-to-end. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: editor_export_popup_focus_trap
criteria.description: The Detail/Editor panel, Export drawer, batch-delete confirmation, and the subscribe popup use dialog semantics: focus moves into them on open, stays trapped while open, and returns to the invoking control on close.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Export focus trapping was observed, but closing returned focus to the page body rather than a verified invoking control, and the other required dialogs were not fully exercised. Evidence: editor_export_popup_focus_trap-shared.png

criteria.name: chrome_icons_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Icon-only chrome controls (MENU, CART, favorite flags, undo/redo, and similar) expose descriptive accessible names or labels so assistive technology can identify them.
criteria.reasoning: Menu, cart, favorite, undo, redo, and related icon controls expose descriptive accessible names. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: swatch_and_export_copy_announced_live
criteria.description: Copying a swatch's hex and copying export preview text announce the confirmation through an aria-live polite region in addition to the visible label.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Export-copy confirmation was announced through aria-live, but swatch-copy could not be exercised because the coachmark intercepted interaction. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: editor_subscribe_import_fields_labeled
criteria.description: Every palette create/edit field (name, artist, period, swatches), the subscribe email field, and the archive import field uses an explicit label element associated with the control.
criteria.reasoning: Explicit labels were observed for editor name, artist, period, swatches, subscribe email, and archive import controls. Evidence: contrast_matrix_not_color_only-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Archive headings (editorial intro through library sections and export) follow a logical order with no skips such as H1 jumping directly to H3.
criteria.reasoning: Observed heading sequence begins with H1, proceeds through H2 sections, and uses H3 only beneath dialog sections without skips. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: landmarks_present
criteria.description: The page exposes semantic landmarks (for example main for the library and complementary/dialog for Detail/Editor, Export, or subscribe) so assistive technology can navigate the chrome.
criteria.reasoning: Observed nav, main, footer, regions, and dialog landmarks with accessible labels or labelled-by associations. Evidence: editor_export_popup_focus_trap-shared.png

criteria.name: auto_contrast_swatch_labels
criteria.description: Auto-contrast swatch labels keep legible contrast against every swatch color, including very light and very dark swatches, and chrome text meets sufficient contrast on the cream field.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The labeled swatch presentation across all light and dark colors could not be fully reached and verified visually. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_roles_for_controls
criteria.description: View toggles, period filter, name sort, favorite flags, batch actions, and primary actions use semantic HTML roles such as button, and the page uses nav/main landmarks rather than unlabeled div soup for structure.
criteria.reasoning: View toggles, filters, favorites, batch actions, primary actions, nav, and main use semantic buttons/inputs and landmarks; facet state uses aria-pressed. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, scroll reveals and list animations are removed and state changes apply instantly while every feature (views, filter, sort, editor, export, copy, favorites, vision simulation) stays reachable — verified on a fresh load.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Reduced-motion was confirmed and animation/transition durations collapsed to 0.001s, but all listed features were not exercised end-to-end in that separate browser instance. Evidence: reduced_motion_respected-shared.png

criteria.name: contrast_matrix_not_color_only
criteria.description: Contrast-matrix pass/fail marks include a text label such as Pass or Fail alongside any color cue — not color alone.
criteria.reasoning: Contrast results visibly include text such as AA Pass, AA Fail, AAA Pass, and AAA Fail alongside the matrix. Evidence: contrast_matrix_not_color_only-shared.png

criteria.name: reorder_keyboard_alternative
criteria.description: Each swatch offers keyboard-operable move-up / move-down controls that produce the same order changes as dragging
criteria.reasoning: The live editor exposes keyboard-focusable Move swatch N up and Move swatch N down buttons for every swatch. Evidence: contrast_matrix_not_color_only-shared.png

criteria.name: facet_and_compare_controls_labeled
criteria.description: The search input, tag facet chips, Archived toggle, Compare control, notes Write/Preview toggle, and catalog Print control are keyboard-operable and labeled, and facet chips expose their selected state to assistive technology rather than through color alone
criteria.reasoning: Search has an explicit label; tag facets, Archived, Compare, Write/Preview, and Print are semantic labeled buttons, with facet selection exposed through aria-pressed. Evidence: keyboard_operable_archive_controls-shared.png

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed
criteria.description: Starting from Browse: switch to Palette view, apply a non-all period filter, switch Name sort to Z–A, create a valid palette, toggle a favorite, select two cards, set vision simulation to Deuteranopia, open Export, and open Detail/Editor; then reload. Confirm every facet coherently resets to the seeded baseline — seeded palettes only, Nomenclature active, all-periods filter, Name A–Z, empty undo/redo, vision None, no selection — never a mix of surviving and reverted facets.
criteria.reasoning: Reload reset the library to 9 seeded palettes, all-periods filter, Name A–Z, vision None, no selections, and no open dialogs. Evidence: multi_facet_reload_resets_to_seed-shared.png

criteria.name: name_sort_reversal_proves_live_list
criteria.description: Sort-reversal proof: with at least 6 named palettes visible in Palette view, choose Name A–Z and record the exact card title order, then choose Name Z–A. Confirm the order reverses correctly relative to ascending, proving sort is derived from live data rather than two hardcoded orders.
criteria.reasoning: The 10-card ascending order reversed exactly under Name Z–A. Evidence: name_sort_reversal_proves_live_list-shared.png

criteria.name: period_filter_derived_view_sensitivity
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: with mixed period tags present, switch the period filter from all → a specific period → another period → all. Confirm visible membership in Nomenclature, Palette, and Swatch changes meaningfully for each input rather than redrawing an identical set regardless of filter.
criteria.reasoning: Counts changed across periods and all three views: Nomenclature 50→11→10, Palette 10→2→2, Swatch 51→11→10. Evidence: period_filter_derived_view_sensitivity-shared.png

criteria.name: edit_echoes_across_layouts_and_export
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: in Detail/Editor rename a palette and change its period; close to Browse and open Export without reload. Confirm the Palette-view card title, Nomenclature source title column, period-filter membership, and archive JSON export all reflect the edit.
criteria.reasoning: Renamed a palette and changed its period; the renamed card, nomenclature sources, Modern filter membership, and JSON export reflected the changes. Evidence: edit_echoes_across_layouts_and_export-shared.png

criteria.name: create_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure the visible palette count, create one valid palette via the UI, and re-measure immediately — the count increases by exactly one (no off-by-one, no lagging counter).
criteria.reasoning: Visible count changed from 9 to exactly 10 after creating UI Real. Evidence: create_count_delta_exact-shared.png

criteria.name: different_inputs_different_palettes_and_exports
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: create two valid palettes with different names and distinct swatch hex sets; confirm both appear with those distinct names/swatches and the export JSON/CSS previews differ appropriately rather than identical hardcoded output.
criteria.reasoning: Created UI Real and UI Green with distinct names and hex sets; both appeared and JSON contained distinct swatch arrays. Evidence: different_inputs_different_palettes_and_exports-shared.png

criteria.name: interleaved_create_filter_and_export
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: start creating a palette (open editor, enter a name), switch to Swatch view and apply a period filter, open Export on another format tab, return and finish creating a second valid palette; confirm the filter state persisted, exactly one new completed palette appears, and export previews include it without corrupting the other flow's state.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The required interleaved create/filter/export workflow was not completed end-to-end and no verified second-flow result was observed. Evidence: export_import_round_trip_integrity-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_then_repopulate_library_and_export
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete every palette (empty state visible; export still compiles), then create two valid palettes; confirm the empty state clears, both new cards/index entries appear across layouts, period filtering operates on the repopulated collection, and export JSON lists both new palettes.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The complete delete-all, visible empty-state, repopulation, filtering, and export workflow was not observed end-to-end. Evidence: export_import_round_trip_integrity-shared.png

criteria.name: batch_delete_undo_export_pipeline
criteria.description: Full pipeline: select two palettes, confirm Batch delete (count −2, both absent from archive JSON), then Undo — both palettes return, count restores, and export JSON again contains both names and swatches.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Batch deletion was exercised, but Undo did not restore the deleted palette set in the observed result. Evidence: export_import_round_trip_integrity-shared.png

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_integrity
criteria.description: Mutate the collection (create a palette with a unique hex), Copy the archive JSON export text, then Import that text: visible library state and the export preview match the mutated pre-export state (round-trip).
criteria.reasoning: Copied the visible Archive JSON preview into the Import archive textarea; import reported success and the library/export still matched, including the renamed palette and #FFFFFF swatch. Evidence: export_import_round_trip_integrity-shared.png

criteria.name: contrast_matrix_input_dependent
criteria.description: Open Detail/Editor for a palette with ≥3 swatches, note a contrast ratio for a pair, recolor one of those swatches to a clearly different luminance, and confirm that pair's ratio and/or pass/fail mark changes.
criteria.reasoning: Recolored a swatch from #2C3E50 to #FFFFFF; the contrast pair list and ratios changed. Evidence: contrast_matrix_input_dependent-shared.png

criteria.name: api_shaped_create_to_export_pipeline
criteria.description: Full pipeline: create a valid palette with distinctive name, artist, closed-list period, and unique hex swatches; confirm the Palette-view card and Nomenclature source show name/artist; open Export JSON and confirm version palette-archive.v1 plus the new palette's id, name, artist, period, swatches, and favorite are present; Copy then Import that JSON and confirm the library and export preview still match.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Creation and JSON export fields were observed, but the same distinctive create-to-copy-to-import workflow was not completed end-to-end. Evidence: different_inputs_different_palettes_and_exports-shared.png

criteria.name: reorder_export_pipeline
criteria.description: Record a palette's swatch order from the archive JSON preview, reorder one swatch via the UI, and confirm the swatches array order in a fresh export changes to match the visible order; a second, different reorder yields a correspondingly different exported order
criteria.reasoning: Moving swatch 2 up changed the exported array order, and a second move produced a different corresponding order. Evidence: reorder_export_pipeline-shared.png

criteria.name: tag_search_derived_sensitivity
criteria.description: Add a distinctive tag to one palette, confirm searching that tag or activating its facet chip isolates exactly that palette, then remove the tag and confirm the same search matches nothing and the chip count updates
criteria.reasoning: A distinctive tag search isolated exactly one visible card; after removing the tag, the same search showed zero visible cards and no tag chip. Evidence: tag_search_derived_sensitivity-shared.png

criteria.name: notes_edit_catalog_pipeline
criteria.description: Two different provenance notes saved on the same palette produce two different rendered catalog-sheet entries and two different notes strings in the archive JSON export
criteria.reasoning: Two saved provenance notes produced different catalog-sheet content and JSON notes strings; the second export contained only the second note. Evidence: notes_edit_catalog_pipeline-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_matches_editorial_scale
criteria.description: At 1440px, spacing among the sticky header, editorial intro, library controls, and nomenclature rows follows a consistent editorial scale matching the reference screenshots — no arbitrary one-off gaps that break the archive composition.
criteria.reasoning: Consistent margins, section spacing, controls, and hairline row rhythm are visible at desktop width. Evidence: nomenclature-full.png

criteria.name: typography_matches_expressive_pairing
criteria.description: Titles use expressive display pairing, hex meta uses mono, and historical color names use italic treatment consistent with the visual spec and reference screenshots — not a single default UI stack for all of those roles.
criteria.reasoning: Display headings use Libre Baskerville, hex values use IBM Plex Mono, and historical names are italicized. Evidence: nomenclature-full.png

criteria.name: desktop_composition_matches_reference
criteria.description: At 1440px the layout matches the reference composition within a small tolerance: sticky script/MENU/CART chrome, editorial intro above the library, view toggles with circular indicators, period filter on the right, and four-column nomenclature rows with hairline rules.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop view shows sticky chrome, editorial intro, view toggles, period filter, and four-column nomenclature rows with rules. Evidence: nomenclature-full.png

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_have_motion

criteria.name: controls_styled_not_browser_default

criteria.name: clear_type_hierarchy

criteria.reasoning: The display headline, section labels, italic historical names, monospace hex metadata, and export preview are visibly differentiated. Evidence: nomenclature-full.png, export-drawer.png

criteria.name: view_toggle_and_hover_states_match_spec

criteria.reasoning: Active view uses a filled indicator, inactive views are outline-only, and real hover inspection showed swatch outline wash changes. Evidence: palette-view.png

criteria.name: cream_field_and_hairline_treatments
criteria.description: Cream editorial field, near-black foreground, and hairline rules on nomenclature rows match the specified surface treatments rather than a dashboard card-grid look.
criteria.reasoning: The page uses a cream field, near-black foreground, and repeated hairline rules across nomenclature rows. Evidence: nomenclature-full.png

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_duration
criteria.description: Microinteraction feedback for swatch copy (~1s clear), favorite toggle, export copy confirmation, and hover outlines follows the spec in duration and motion when exercised through the real UI path.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Swatch hover has timed transitions, but favorite feedback reports no transition and the complete requested set of copy/favorite/hover duration behaviors was not observed as specified. Evidence: palette-view.png

criteria.name: export_and_contrast_surfaces_match_spec

criteria.name: headings_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles (editorial intro, library controls, Detail/Editor, Export drawer), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout.
criteria.reasoning: Sentence-case headings are used consistently across the index, editor, contrast sections, and export drawer. Evidence: headings_consistent_capitalization-shared.png; facet_comparison_catalog_labels-shared.png; contrast_and_export_headers_specific-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific verbs appropriate to the archive (for example Batch delete, Copy export, Undo, Duplicate) rather than generic Submit/OK alone when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Observed specific labels including Create palette, Export, Compare, Duplicate, Delete palette, Save palette, Copy export, Undo, and Batch delete. Evidence: facet_comparison_catalog_labels-shared.png

criteria.name: validation_names_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders validation messages for palette create/edit (name, artist, period, swatches), subscribe email, or archive import field-contract failures, they name the field and the fix, not only a bare rejection like 'Invalid'.
criteria.reasoning: Create-palette validation explicitly names fields and fixes: name required/title, artist required/credit source, period required/choose a period, and swatch requirements. Evidence: validation_names_field_and_fix-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_library_copy_explains_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders an empty library state (after delete-all or a zero-match period filter), the copy explains what belongs there and how to start a new palette or clear the filter.
criteria.reasoning: Zero-match filtering displays “No palettes match period: Fauvism” and explains clearing active filters, with a Clear all filters action. Evidence: empty_library_copy_explains_next_step-shared.png
